Journal.Title: Liver international : official journal of the International Association for the Study of the Liver

Article.ArticleTitle: Indocyanine Green Clearance Test via Pulse Dye Densitometry for Portal Hypertension Diagnosis in cACLD.

Abstract.AbstractText: Performance of pulse dye densitometry (PDD)-estimated indocyanine green 15-min retention rate (ICG-R15) in the diagnosis of clinically significant portal hypertension (CSPH) has not been studied. A prospective compensated advanced chronic liver disease (cACLD) cohort was used to explore the capability of ICG-R15 in the differentiation of CSPH as referenced by hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG).

Performance of ICG-R15 alone and combined with Baveno VII algorithm for CSPH differentiation was established with a monocentric set and validated with a bicentric set of patients with HVPG assessment.

In the training set (n = 96), ICG-R15 ≥ 14% could identify 63.6% (28/44) CSPH with 96.2% specificity and 93.3% positive predictive value (PPV). ICG-R15 < 4% was able to exclude 40.4% (21/52) non-CSPH with 95.5% sensitivity and 91.3% negative predictive value (NPV). In the validation set (n = 106), ICG-R15 ≥ 14% could identify 63.6% (28/44) CSPH with 95.2% specificity and 90.3% PPV. ICG-R15 < 4% could exclude 41.9% (26/62) non-CSPH with 95.5% sensitivity and 92.9% NPV. Sensitivity analysis in the total set of patients (n = 202) reinforced robustness. Combined ICG-R15 ≥ 14% with LSM ≥ 25 kPa improved performance with 72.7% (64/88) sensitivity, 85.3% PPV and 90.4% specificity. Meanwhile, ICG-R15 < 4% with LSM ≤ 15 kPa plus platelet count ≥ 150 × 109/L excluded CSPH with 95.5% sensitivity and 94.5% NPV, reducing diagnosis uncertainty (grey zone) to 26.7%.

PDD-estimated ICG-R15 ≥ 14% could identify CSPH and ICG-R15 < 4% to exclude CSPH in cACLD patients. ICG-R15 sequential with Baveno VII enhanced diagnostic capability, warranting multicentre validation.

Reference.Citation: C. Villanueva, A. Albillos, J. Genescà, et al., “β Blockers to Prevent Decompensation of Cirrhosis in Patients With Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension (PREDESCI): A Randomised, Double‐Blind, Placebo‐Controlled, Multicentre Trial,” Lancet 393, no. 10181 (2019): 1597–1608.
R. de Franchis, J. Bosch, G. Garcia‐Tsao, et al., “Baveno VII ‐ Renewing Consensus in Portal Hypertension,” Journal of Hepatology 76, no. 4 (2022): 959–974.

R. He, C. Liu, I. Grgurevic, et al., “Validation of Baveno VII Criteria for Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension by Two‐Dimensional Shear Wave Elastography,” Hepatology International 18, no. 3 (2024): 1020–1028.
I. Gupta, J. R. Eisenbrey, P. Machado, et al., “Diagnosing Portal Hypertension With Noninvasive Subharmonic Pressure Estimates From a US Contrast Agent,” Radiology 298, no. 1 (2021): 104–111.

Y. Liu, T. Tang, N. Ormeci, et al., “Noncontrast‐Enhanced MRI‐Based Noninvasive Score for Portal Hypertension (CHESS1802): An International Multicenter Study,” Journal of Clinical and Translational Hepatology 9, no. 6 (2021): 818–827.

D. Catucci, V. C. Obmann, A. Berzigotti, et al., “Noninvasive Assessment of Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension Using ΔT1 of the Liver and Spleen and ECV of the Spleen on Routine Gd‐EOB‐DTPA Liver MRI,” European Journal of Radiology 144 (2021): 109958.
S. J. Hectors, O. Bane, D. Stocker, et al., “Splenic T(1rho) as a Noninvasive Biomarker for Portal Hypertension,” Journal of Magnetic Resonance Imaging 52, no. 3 (2020): 787–794.
R. Sartoris, P. E. Rautou, L. Elkrief, et al., “Quantification of Liver Surface Nodularity at CT: Utility for Detection of Portal Hypertension,” Radiology 289, no. 3 (2018): 698–707.
C. Wang, Y. Huang, C. Liu, et al., “Diagnosis of Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension Using CT‐ and MRI‐Based Vascular Model,” Radiology 307, no. 2 (2023): e221648.

B. Simbrunner, R. Marculescu, B. Scheiner, et al., “Non‐Invasive Detection of Portal Hypertension by Enhanced Liver Fibrosis Score in Patients With Different Aetiologies of Advanced Chronic Liver Disease,” Liver International 40, no. 7 (2020): 1713–1724.

M. Ferlitsch, T. Reiberger, M. Hoke, et al., “von Willebrand Factor as New Noninvasive Predictor of Portal Hypertension, Decompensation and Mortality in Patients With Liver Cirrhosis,” Hepatology 56, no. 4 (2012): 1439–1447.

A. Lisotti, F. Azzaroli, F. Buonfiglioli, et al., “Indocyanine Green Retention Test as a Noninvasive Marker of Portal Hypertension and Esophageal Varices in Compensated Liver Cirrhosis,” Hepatology 59, no. 2 (2014): 643–650.

A. Lisotti, F. Azzaroli, A. Cucchetti, et al., “Relationship Between Indocyanine Green Retention Test, Decompensation and Survival in Patients With Child‐Pugh A Cirrhosis and Portal Hypertension,” Liver International 36, no. 9 (2016): 1313–1321.

K. Kobayashi, E. Uldry, T. Kokudo, et al., “Correlation Between Portal Pressure and Indocyanine Green Retention Rate Is Unaffected by the Cause of Cirrhosis: A Prospective Study,” World Journal of Surgery 45, no. 8 (2021): 2546–2555.

S. Møller, E. la Cour Sibbesen, J. L. Madsen, and F. Bendtsen, “Indocyanine Green Retention Test in Cirrhosis and Portal Hypertension: Accuracy and Relation to Severity of Disease,” Journal of Gastroenterology and Hepatology 34, no. 6 (2019): 1093–1099.

M. L. Pind, F. Bendtsen, T. Kallemose, M.‐L. L. Pind, and S. Møller, “Indocyanine Green Retention Test (ICG‐r15) as a Noninvasive Predictor of Portal Hypertension in Patients With Different Severity of Cirrhosis,” European Journal of Gastroenterology & Hepatology 28, no. 8 (2016): 948–954.

T. T. Cheung, S. C. Chan, K. S. Chok, et al., “Rapid Measurement of Indocyanine Green Retention by Pulse Spectrophotometry: A Validation Study in 70 Patients With Child‐Pugh A Cirrhosis Before Hepatectomy for Hepatocellular Carcinoma,” Hepatobiliary & Pancreatic Diseases International 11, no. 3 (2012): 267–271.

O. Okochi, T. Kaneko, H. Sugimoto, S. Inoue, S. Takeda, and A. Nakao, “ICG Pulse Spectrophotometry for Perioperative Liver Function in Hepatectomy,” Journal of Surgical Research 103, no. 1 (2002): 109–113.
X. Shu, H. Sun, X. Yang, et al., “Correlation of Effective Hepatic Blood Flow With Liver Pathology in Patients With Hepatitis B Virus,” Liver Research 5, no. 4 (2021): 243–250.

D. Chen, S. Wen, Z. Wu, et al., “Association Between Effective Hepatic Blood Flow and the Severity and Prognosis of Hepatitis B Virus‐Related Acute on Chronic Liver Failure,” European Journal of Gastroenterology & Hepatology 32, no. 2 (2021): 246–254.

D. Zhang, Y. Pan, Z. Yang, et al., “A Nomogram Based on Preoperative Lab Tests, BMI, ICG‐R15, and EHBF for the Prediction of Post‐Hepatectomy Liver Failure in Patients With Hepatocellular Carcinoma,” Journal of Clinical Medicine 12, no. 1 (2022): 324.

X. Zhang, J. Song, Y. Zhang, et al., “Baveno VII Algorithm Outperformed Other Models in Ruling out High‐Risk Varices in Individuals With HBV‐Related Cirrhosis,” Journal of Hepatology 78, no. 3 (2023): 574–583.

R. de Franchis and V. I. F. Baveno, “Expanding Consensus in Portal Hypertension: Report of the Baveno VI Consensus Workshop: Stratifying Risk and Individualizing Care for Portal Hypertension,” Journal of Hepatology 63, no. 3 (2015): 743–752.

J. Bosch, J. G. Abraldes, A. Berzigotti, and J. C. García‐Pagan, “The Clinical Use of HVPG Measurements in Chronic Liver Disease,” Nature Reviews. Gastroenterology & Hepatology 6, no. 10 (2009): 573–582.
K. C. B. Tan, “Appropriate Body‐Mass Index for Asian Populations and Its Implications for Policy and Intervention Strategies,” Lancet 363, no. 9403 (2004): 157–163.

H. Wang, B. Wen, X. Chang, et al., “Baveno VI Criteria and Spleen Stiffness Measurement Rule out High‐Risk Varices in Virally Suppressed HBV‐Related Cirrhosis,” Journal of Hepatology 74, no. 3 (2021): 584–592.
J. H. Kao, T. H. Hu, J. Jia, et al., “East Asia Expert Opinion on Treatment Initiation for Chronic Hepatitis B,” Alimentary Pharmacology & Therapeutics 52, no. 10 (2020): 1540–1550.

P. Martin, M. H. Nguyen, D. T. Dieterich, et al., “Treatment Algorithm for Managing Chronic Hepatitis B Virus Infection in the United States: 2021 Update,” Clinical Gastroenterology and Hepatology 20, no. 8 (2022): 1766–1775.

E. Dajti, F. Ravaioli, G. Marasco, et al., “A Combined Baveno VII and Spleen Stiffness Algorithm to Improve the Noninvasive Diagnosis of Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension in Patients With Compensated Advanced Chronic Liver Disease,” American Journal of Gastroenterology 117, no. 11 (2022): 1825–1833.

F. Primavesi, M. Maglione, F. Cipriani, et al., “E‐AHPBA‐ESSO‐ESSR Innsbruck Consensus Guidelines for Preoperative Liver Function Assessment Before Hepatectomy,” British Journal of Surgery 110, no. 10 (2023): 1331–1347.

M. Caimano, G. Bianco, A. Coppola, et al., “Indocyanine Green Clearance Tests to Assess Liver Transplantation Outcomes: A Systematic Review,” International Journal of Surgery (London, England) 110, no. 1 (2024): 431–440.
M. T. Inal, D. Memiş, M. Kargi, et al., “Prognostic Value of Indocyanine Green Elimination Assessed With LiMON in Septic Patients,” Journal of Critical Care 24, no. 3 (2009): 329–334.

B. M. Halle, T. D. Poulsen, and H. P. Pedersen, “Indocyanine Green Plasma Disappearance Rate as Dynamic Liver Function Test in Critically Ill Patients,” Acta Anaesthesiologica Scandinavica 58, no. 10 (2014): 1214–1219.

F. Haertel, S. Nuding, D. Reisberg, et al., “The Prognostic Value of a Liver Function Test Using Indocyanine Green (ICG) Clearance in Patients With Multiple Organ Dysfunction Syndrome (MODS),” Journal of Clinical Medicine 13, no. 4 (2024): 1039.
W. de Graaf, S. Häusler, M. Heger, et al., “Transporters Involved in the Hepatic Uptake of (99m) Tc‐Mebrofenin and Indocyanine Green,” Journal of Hepatology 54, no. 4 (2011): 738–745.

M. Pons, S. Augustin, B. Scheiner, et al., “Noninvasive Diagnosis of Portal Hypertension in Patients With Compensated Advanced Chronic Liver Disease,” American Journal of Gastroenterology 116, no. 4 (2021): 723–732.

M. Jachs, L. Hartl, B. Simbrunner, et al., “The Sequential Application of Baveno VII Criteria and VITRO Score Improves Diagnosis of Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension,” Clinical Gastroenterology and Hepatology 21, no. 7 (2023): 1854–1863.e10.

M. Jachs, A. Odriozola, F. Turon, et al., “Spleen Stiffness Measurement by Vibration‐Controlled Transient Elastography at 100 Hz for Non‐Invasive Predicted Diagnosis of Clinically Significant Portal Hypertension in Patients With Compensated Advanced Chronic Liver Disease: A Modelling Study,” Lancet Gastroenterology & Hepatology 9, no. 12 (2024): 1111–1120.

B. Y. Liang, E. L. Zhang, J. Li, et al., “A Combined Pre‐ and Intra‐Operative Nomogram in Evaluation of Degrees of Liver Cirrhosis Predicts Post‐Hepatectomy Liver Failure: A Multicenter Prospective Study,” Hepatobiliary Surgery and Nutrition 13, no. 2 (2024): 198–213.

H. L. Feng, Q. Li, L. Wang, G. Y. Yuan, and W. K. Cao, “Indocyanine Green Clearance Test Combined With MELD Score in Predicting the Short‐Term Prognosis of Patients With Acute Liver Failure,” Hepatobiliary & Pancreatic Diseases International 13, no. 3 (2014): 271–275.
